---
title: "RANDOM FOREST CLASSIFICATION & SHAP SUMMARY FOR LANDSLIDE SUSCEPTIBILITY MAPPING::JAMMU REGION"
author: "Mewan Banshan War (mewan.banshan@research.iiit.ac.in)"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
--- 

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(tidyverse)
library(exactextractr)
library(raster)

# Set working directory from which to load the AOI (here, Jammu) shapefile
setwd("D:/IGARSS 2026/Datasets")

# AOI
Jammu <- st_read("Jammu_region.shp")
```

```{r}
# Load the 12 reclassified input thematic raster layers
elevation <- rast("Elev_Reclassified.tif")
slope <- rast("Slop_Reclassified.tif")
aspect <- rast("Aspec_Reclassified.tif")
curvature <- rast("Curv_Reclassified.tif")
lithology <- rast("Lithology_Re_Re_classified.tif")
geomorph <- rast("Geomorph_Reclassified.tif")
lulc <- rast("LULC_Reclassified.tif")
rainfall <- rast("Rain_Reclassified.tif")
dist_river <- rast("Reclass_Fill_Riv_Clip.tif")
lineament <- rast("Lineament_Reclassified.tif")
fault <- rast("Fault_Reclassified.tif")
soil_text <- rast("Soil_Text_Re_Reclassified.tif")
```


```{r}
#### ENSURING ALL EXTENTS MATCH #################
library(terra)
library(corrplot)

# Combine all rasters in a list
raster_list <- list(
  elevation, slope, aspect, curvature, lithology,
  geomorph, lulc, rainfall, dist_river,
  lineament, fault, soil_text
)

# Clip each raster to Jammu (with memory safety)
clip_to_Jammu <- function(r) {
  # Use on-disk temp files to reduce RAM load
  r <- crop(r, Jammu, filename = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"), overwrite = TRUE)
  r <- mask(r, Jammu, filename = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"), overwrite = TRUE)
  return(r)
}

clipped_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(raster_list)) {
  cat("Processing:", i, "/", length(raster_list), "\n")
  clipped_list[[i]] <- clip_to_Jammu(raster_list[[i]])
  gc()  # release memory after each raster
}

# Assign names for clarity
names(clipped_list) <- c(
  "elevation", "slope", "aspect", "curvature", "lithology",
  "geomorph", "lulc", "rainfall", "dist_river",
  "lineament", "fault", "soil_text"
)

# Use 'elevation' as reference raster
ref <- clipped_list[[1]]

# Align all rasters to the reference grid 
align_to_ref <- function(r) {
  # Reproject if CRS differs
  if (!compareGeom(ref, r, stopOnError = FALSE, crs = TRUE)) {
    r <- project(r, ref)
  }
  return(r)
}

aligned_list <- lapply(clipped_list, align_to_ref)

# Stack all rasters together
predictors <- rast(aligned_list)
names(predictors) <- names(clipped_list)
print(predictors)
```
```{r}
##### MULTICOLLINEARITY ANALYSIS ###################
# Randomly sample 1000 pixels
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Store to a dataframe
sample_df <- spatSample(predictors, size = 1000, method = "random", na.rm = TRUE, as.data.frame = TRUE)

# 1. Compute Spearman correlation coefficient (Highly correlated: |r|>=0.8)
cor_matrix <- cor(sample_df, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")

# Plot the correlation matrix
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color",
         type = "full",
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.cex = 0.8,
         addCoef.col = "black",
         number.cex = 0.6,
         title = "Spearman’s Correlation Matrix (1000 random pixels)",
         mar = c(0,0,2,0))

## VARIANCE INFLATION FACTOR (VIF<0.5) + TOLERANCE VALUES (>0.2) => NON-COLLINEARITY
# Load necessary package
library(car)

# Remove NA values
sample_df_clean <- na.omit(sample_df)

# Create a dummy response variable
sample_df_clean$dummy <- runif(nrow(sample_df_clean))

# Fit a  linear model using all predictors to get VIF values
model <- lm(dummy ~ ., data = sample_df_clean)

# Compute VIF
vif_values <- vif(model)

# Compute Tolerance = 1 / VIF
tolerance_values <- 1 / vif_values

# Combine in one table
vif_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(vif_values),
  VIF = round(vif_values, 3),
  Tolerance = round(tolerance_values, 3)
)
print(vif_table)
```


```{r}
##### RANDOM FOREST MODEL TRAINING ###################################

# Load the valid landslide points
ls_pts <- st_read("Landslide_All_Features_reproj.shp")

# Load the non-landslide points
nls_pts <- st_read("New_NLS_Points_LL.shp")

```
```{r}
# Ensure same CRS between points and predictors
ls_pts <- st_transform(ls_pts, crs(predictors))
nls_pts <- st_transform(nls_pts, crs(predictors))

# Extract predictor values at landslide points 
ls_data <- terra::extract(predictors, vect(ls_pts), xy = TRUE)
ls_data$landslide <- 1  # assign response variable (landslide = 1)

# Extract predictor values at non-landslide points 
nls_data <- terra::extract(predictors, vect(nls_pts), xy = TRUE)
nls_data$landslide <- 0  # assign response variable (landslide = 0)

# Equalizing the number of valid landslide & random non-landslide samples within the study area
set.seed(123)
n <- min(nrow(ls_data), nrow(nls_data))
ls_bal <- ls_data[sample(1:nrow(ls_data), n), ]
nls_bal <- nls_data[sample(1:nrow(nls_data), n), ]

# Combine both datasets 
data_all <- rbind(ls_bal, nls_bal)
data_all <- na.omit(data_all)  # remove NA rows

# Train-test split (70:30) on the combined dataset to PREVENT DATA LEAKAGE
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(data_all$landslide, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_set <- data_all[train_index, ]
test_set  <- data_all[-train_index, ]

# Train a Random Forest classifier model with 500 trees
rf_model <- randomForest(
  factor(landslide) ~ elevation + slope + aspect + curvature + lithology +
    geomorph + lulc + rainfall + dist_river + lineament + fault + soil_text,
  data = train_set,
  ntree = 500,
  mtry = 10,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(rf_model)
```

```{r}
# Visualise a Variable Importance plot
varImpPlot(rf_model)
```

```{r}
##### MODEL VALIDATION #####################
# Evaluate model performance using ROC Curve
# Predict probabilities for test set: predictions between 0 and 1
pred_test <- predict(rf_model, test_set, type = "prob")[, 2]
# selects the second column from that matrix — the predicted probability of the positive class (“1”)

# Load necessary library
library(pROC)

# ROC and AUC
roc_obj <- roc(test_set$landslide, pred_test) # model evaluation using test set
auc_value <- auc(roc_obj)
cat("AUC:", auc_value, "\n")

# Identify the best threshold
best_coords <- coords(
  roc_obj, 
  "best",
  ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity")
)

# Convert specificity → FPR
best_coords$false_positive_rate <- 1 - best_coords$specificity
best_coords$true_positive_rate  <- best_coords$sensitivity
best_coords <- best_coords[, c("threshold", "true_positive_rate", "false_positive_rate")]
print(best_coords)

# Plot
plot <- plot(
  1 - roc_obj$specificities, roc_obj$sensitivities,
  type = "l", lwd = 3, col = "blue",
  xlab = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
  ylab = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
  main = paste("ROC Curve for LSI using RF")
)

# Add 1:1 reference line (random prediction)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "gray")

# Add AUC text manually
text(
  x = 0.55, y = 0.4,
  labels = paste("AUC =", round(auc_value, 3)),
  col = "blue", cex = 1, font = 1
)

# Create an ROC dataframe
roc_data <- data.frame(
  Threshold = roc_obj$thresholds,
  True_Positive_Rate = roc_obj$sensitivities,
  False_Positive_Rate = 1 - roc_obj$specificities,
  Specificity = roc_obj$specificities
)

# Structural check
head(roc_data)

# Load necessary library
library(openxlsx)

write.xlsx(
  roc_data,
  file = "ROC_Curve_Data_RF.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE
)

```

```{r}
## SHAP Analysis

# Explainability libraries
library(fastshap)
library(iml)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Predictor matrix (training data only → NO data leakage)
X_train <- train_set %>%
  dplyr::select(
    elevation, slope, aspect, curvature, lithology,
    geomorph, lulc, rainfall, dist_river,
    lineament, fault, soil_text
  )

# Response variable
y_train <- train_set$landslide

# Probability prediction function (positive class)
pred_fun <- function(object, newdata) {
  predict(object, newdata = newdata, type = "prob")[, 2]
}

# Set the seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Compute SHAP values for each RF-based predictor variable
shap_values <- fastshap::explain(
  object       = rf_model,
  X            = X_train,
  pred_wrapper = pred_fun,
  nsim         = 100,
  adjust       = TRUE
)

# Convert to long format
shap_long <- as.data.table(shap_values)
shap_long[, id := 1:.N]

shap_long <- melt(
  shap_long,
  id.vars = "id",
  variable.name = "Feature",
  value.name = "SHAP_value"
)

# Add original feature values
X_long <- as.data.table(X_train)
X_long[, id := 1:.N]

X_long <- melt(
  X_long,
  id.vars = "id",
  variable.name = "Feature",
  value.name = "Feature_value"
)

# Merge
shap_long <- merge(shap_long, X_long, by = c("id", "Feature"))
```


```{r}
 # Plot a SHAP Summary Beeswarm plot
library(ggbeeswarm)

summ_plot <- ggplot(
  shap_long,
  aes(
  x = SHAP_value,
  y = reorder(Feature, abs(SHAP_value), FUN = mean),
  color = Feature_value
    )
  ) +
  
  # Zero reference line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  # Beeswarm-style density spreading
  geom_quasirandom(
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 1.3,
    width = 0.35
    ) +

  # Colour gradient (Low to High only)
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "plasma",
    breaks = c(min(shap_long$Feature_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(shap_long$Feature_value, na.rm = TRUE)),
    labels = c("Low", "High")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "SHAP Value (Impact on Landslide Probability)",
    y = "Conditioning Factors",
    color = "Feature value"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9), # Put this vertically
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 9)
  )

print(summ_plot)
```
